message:
fix(mvcc/ebr): handle out-of-order epoch retire batching and non-contiguous drain

The EbrRetireQueue assumed batches arrived in monotonically increasing epoch
order and that all reclaimable batches formed a contiguous prefix. Both
assumptions break under concurrent writers where transaction commit ordering
diverges from epoch advancement:

1. retire() now uses binary_search_by_key to insert into the sorted pending
   list, coalescing indices into an existing batch when a duplicate epoch
   arrives. This eliminates the silent misordering that could cause a batch
   to sit behind a newer epoch and never be drained.

2. drain_if_safe() replaces take_while (which stopped at the first
   non-reclaimable batch) with a filter + partition that separates
   reclaimable from retained batches regardless of position. This ensures
   an older reclaimable batch isn't stranded behind a younger one whose
   epoch hasn't yet crossed the safe horizon.

Adds test ebr_retire_queue_out_of_order_epochs covering the scenario where
epoch 5 is retired before epoch 3, verifying both batches drain correctly
as the safe horizon advances past each.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
